seoTitle: Springs vs St James — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Springs and St James. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Springs vs St James: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Springs and St James.
heading: Springs vs St James — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Springs, Western Australia and St James, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: St James has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Springs (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Springs or St James safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Springs has a flood risk score of 42/100, while St James scores 30/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Springs scores 51/100 for bushfire risk. St James scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Springs or St James?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. St James has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
